commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=0cde4f7edb0937ea355ee98c0ab5149bfba3ed48)
commands.review_summary:
The PR routes Telegram text `/steer`, `/tell`, and `/queue` commands to the per-chat control sequentialization lane and adds sequential-key unit cases.

PR surface: Source +20, Tests +22. Total +42 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: current main routes abort and read-only status commands to the control lane, while `/steer`, `/tell`, and `/queue` aliases fall through to the normal chat/topic lane. I did not run a live Telegram repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor or maintainer live Telegram proof, a branch refresh over the current sequential-key implementation, and a targeted-command fix before merge.

target.title: [codex] Route Telegram management commands to the control lane
target.body:
## Summary

Routes Telegram `/steer`, `/tell`, and `/queue` text commands onto the existing per-chat control sequentialization lane.

## Why

Telegram updates are sequentialized by chat/thread so normal messages do not race with each other. That is correct for conversational messages, but management commands that are intended to affect an active run should not wait behind the same long-running message they are trying to control.

`/stop` and status commands already bypass the normal chat lane. This gives `/steer`, its `/tell` alias, and `/queue` the same immediate lane behavior.

## Validation

- `vitest run extensions/telegram/src/sequential-key.test.ts`
- `oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/telegram/src/sequential-key.ts extensions/telegram/src/sequential-key.test.ts`
target.author: kyzcreig

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
